preset_description: Dark = identity signal. Visual language expresses who the user is. Validator gets authority and weight (heavy dark). Mover gets minimal light with one dark CTA punch. Explorer gets all-neutral — open, spacious, no urgency.

accent_tokens._note: Brand primary CTA color. Global — not per-preset. Two variants: button on a light/neutral section vs. on a dark section.

beats.name: Orientation
beats.rationale: Establishes immediate context for trial users already familiar with the product, reframing the value proposition around why now is the time to commit. Anchors the page for the Validator who needs to know they're in the right place before evaluating proof.

beats.name: Credibility
beats.rationale: Validators require trust signals early to continue engaging. Placing social proof, customer logos, and authority markers right after orientation satisfies their need to confirm the product is vetted by peers and credible organizations before they invest attention in details.

beats.name: Value
beats.rationale: As trial users, they've experienced the product but may not have explored all capabilities. This beat crystallizes the full value proposition and outcome promises, bridging the gap between their trial experience and the complete paid offering.

beats.name: Evidence
beats.rationale: Required for decision-stage funnels and critical for the Validator archetype. Concrete metrics, case study data, and proof points give the trial user the quantitative justification they need to rationalize the purchase decision they're already considering.

beats.name: Decision
beats.rationale: Required for decision-stage funnels. After evidence has built confidence, presenting pricing and plan comparison gives the Validator a clear, structured choice framework. Positioned here so the purchase feels like a logical conclusion rather than a leap of faith.

beats.name: Conversion
beats.rationale: Placed immediately before Recovery per system rules. The CTA captures the commitment at peak decisional momentum—after proof, value, and pricing have all been presented. For trial users, this is the upgrade prompt with clear action language.

beats.name: Recovery
beats.rationale: Final beat provides secondary paths for Validators who aren't yet fully convinced—links to documentation, a contact-sales option, or extended trial request. Ensures no visitor exits without an alternative route back into the funnel.

sections.name: Navigation
sections.rationale: Global navigation — wayfinding and persistent CTA for the session.

sections.name: Hero
sections.rationale: Establishes immediate context for trial users already familiar with the product, reframing the value proposition around why now is the time to commit. Anchors the page for the Validator who needs to know they're in the right place before evaluating proof.

sections.name: Social proof
sections.rationale: Validators require trust signals early to continue engaging. Placing social proof, customer logos, and authority markers right after orientation satisfies their need to confirm the product is vetted by peers and credible organizations before they invest attention in details.

sections.rationale: As trial users, they've experienced the product but may not have explored all capabilities. This beat crystallizes the full value proposition and outcome promises, bridging the gap between their trial experience and the complete paid offering.

sections.name: Proof points
sections.rationale: Required for decision-stage funnels and critical for the Validator archetype. Concrete metrics, case study data, and proof points give the trial user the quantitative justification they need to rationalize the purchase decision they're already considering.

sections.rationale: Required for decision-stage funnels. After evidence has built confidence, presenting pricing and plan comparison gives the Validator a clear, structured choice framework. Positioned here so the purchase feels like a logical conclusion rather than a leap of faith.

sections.name: CTA
sections.rationale: Placed immediately before Recovery per system rules. The CTA captures the commitment at peak decisional momentum—after proof, value, and pricing have all been presented. For trial users, this is the upgrade prompt with clear action language.

sections.name: Footer
sections.rationale: Page close — brand mark, minimal navigation, and legal.

page.reasoning: Decision-stage conversion pages benefit from minimal variant to strip distractions — 'minimal variant for conversion-focused pages — strip nav_links, keep logo and single CTA only.' The persistent nav CTA is critical for trial users moving toward purchase.

Validator archetype requires immediate trust signals above the fold — 'Validator variant always includes trust_signal.' with-social-proof variant positions LogoStrip directly below CTACluster, establishing credibility before users engage deeper.

slots.headline: Your trial showed you the surface. This is the engine.
slots.subhead: MODE resolves component behavior, copy register, and visual emphasis to match every archetype and funnel stage — without manual variants or configuration overhead.
slots.trust_signal: One-time payment. Lifetime access. No subscription.

page.reasoning: Validator archetype depends on attributed, authentic proof — 'with-photo increases engagement and perceived authenticity' and 'Always include role and company for Validator credibility.' Photo attribution is essential for trial users validating purchase decisions.

slots.quote: We'd been patching the same design system for two years — more tokens, more variants, more QA. MODE is the first thing that made the system smarter instead of bigger. Shipped a full page architecture in under an hour on day one.

page.reasoning: Validator users need outcome-oriented clarity on capabilities — 'Validator benefits from specific, outcome-oriented descriptions — not feature names, benefit statements.' 3-col-icon is the default and scales well for typical SaaS feature articulation.

slots.headline: What the full kit includes
slots.subhead: Every capability works together as a single resolution layer — not a collection of isolated tools.

features.title: Semantic Resolution Engine
features.description: Components resolve the right behavior across four dimensions — archetype, funnel stage, intent state, and context mode — without a single manual override.

features.title: IA Planner Agent
features.description: Go from a brief to a full page architecture in seconds. Powered by Claude Opus, it produces section order, component selection, and slot rationale in one pass.

features.title: Multi-Variant Generation
features.description: One brief produces Mover, Validator, and Explorer variants in parallel. No rewrites, no separate briefs — the right register for every audience, automatically.

features.title: Palette Map
features.description: Visual emphasis resolves per component per funnel stage. Accent weight accumulates semantically toward conversion — no manual assignment, no visual hierarchy guesswork.

features.title: Component Manifest
features.description: 11 components fully specified with zone affinity, slot schemas, and usage intent. The system knows where each component belongs and why — before you write a line of copy.

features.title: Full Ownership, No Lock-In
features.description: MODE lives in your codebase. No runtime dependency, no vendor dashboard, no usage billing. Bring your own Anthropic API key and fork freely — it's yours to extend.

page.reasoning: Validator decision-stage users require sourced proof to validate investment — 'Always pair stats with source attribution in Validator contexts — unsourced stats reduce trust.' 3-stat is the preferred default for high-impact metric clusters.

slots.headline: What teams measure after shipping with MODE

stats.label: Median time from install to first working page architecture
Reduction in manual variant work reported by teams in the first sprint
Runtime dependencies — MODE runs entirely inside your codebase and pipeline

page.reasoning: Trial users at decision stage benefit from clear recommendation anchoring — 'Validator variant includes description and longer feature list.' highlighted variant directs attention to recommended tier, reducing choice paralysis.

slots.description: The complete intelligence layer. Resolution engine, component manifest, palette map, IA planner, multi-variant generation, and Next.js preview UI — owned by your team, forever.
features: Three palette presets, editable token dashboard
Next.js preview UI with palette and label overlays
Lifetime access — no subscription, no lock-in

page.reasoning: Validator archetype requires trust signals at conversion moments — 'Validator variant: include subhead and trust_signal (e.g., 'No credit card required').' centered layout with subhead and trust messaging addresses purchase friction for trial users.

slots.headline: The intelligence layer your design system is missing.
slots.subhead: You've seen it work in trial. The full kit adds the resolution engine, the IA planner, and lifetime ownership — no recurring cost, no vendor dependency.
slots.trust_signal: One-time payment. Yours forever. No subscription required.

page.reasoning: Decision-stage conversion pages benefit from recovery CTAs — 'with-cta variant recaptures departing users on key conversion pages — use on pricing and signup flows.' Captures hesitant trial users before exit.
